extra.message: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) vulnerability. User-controlled 'name' parameter from request query string is concatenated into HTML content and passed directly to Jinja2 Template().render(). An attacker can inject template expressions like {{7*7}} or {{config}} to execute arbitrary code, read sensitive data, or achieve Remote Code Execution.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine

extra.message: Hardcoded credential 'SUPER_SECRET_NAME' found in source code. Credentials should never be hardcoded in application code as they can be exposed through source control, code reviews, or decompilation. Use environment variables or secure secret management systems instead.

Hardcoded credential 'SUPER_SECRET_TOKEN' found in source code. This appears to be an authentication/authorization token hardcoded directly in the configuration file. Tokens should be loaded from environment variables or secure vaults, never committed to source code.

FastAPI application running with debug mode enabled (debug=config.DEBUG). Debug mode can expose sensitive information through detailed error messages, stack traces, and interactive debuggers. This should be disabled in production environments.

Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability. The application makes an HTTP request to a URL controlled by the PUBLIC_IP_SERVICE_URL environment variable. If an attacker can control this environment variable (through container escape, config injection, or supply chain attacks), they can force the server to make requests to internal services, metadata endpoints (like AWS 169.254.169.254), or other restricted networks.
